nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Go to {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
- filter by "Ask HN: Who is hiring?" (important with quotes for full match)
- sort by date
- Chrome Network Tab > find API call > click "Copy as cURL"
- n8n HTTP node -> import cURL and paste 
- I've set the API key as Header Auth so you will have to do the above yourself to make this work

nodes.name: Search for Who is hiring posts

parameters.jsonBody:
{
  "query": "\"Ask HN: Who is hiring\"",
  "analyticsTags": [
    "web"
  ],
  "page": 0,
  "hitsPerPage": 30,
  "minWordSizefor1Typo": 4,
  "minWordSizefor2Typos": 8,
  "advancedSyntax": true,
  "ignorePlurals": false,
  "clickAnalytics": true,
  "minProximity": 7,
  "numericFilters": [],
  "tagFilters": [
    [
      "story"
    ],
    []
  ],
  "typoTolerance": "min",
  "queryType": "prefixNone",
  "restrictSearchableAttributes": [
    "title",
    "comment_text",
    "url",
    "story_text",
    "author"
  ],
  "getRankingInfo": true
}

parameters.content:
## Data Structure

We use Openai GPT-4o-mini to transform the raw data in a unified data structure. Feel free to change this.

```json
{
  "company": "Name of the hiring company",
  "title": "Job title/role being advertised",
  "location": "Work location including remote/hybrid status",
  "type": "Employment type (Full-time, Contract, etc)",
  "salary": "Compensation details if provided",
  "description": "Main job description text including requirements and team info",
  "apply_url": "Direct application/job posting URL",
  "company_url": "Company website or careers page"
}
```

parameters.content:
## Go to {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
- filter by "Ask HN: Who is hiring?" (important with quotes for full match)
- sort by date
- Chrome Network Tab > find API call > click "Copy as cURL"
- n8n HTTP node -> import cURL and paste 
- I've set the API key as Header Auth so you will have to do the above yourself to make this work

## Data Structure

We use Openai GPT-4o-mini to transform the raw data in a unified data structure. Feel free to change this.

```json
{
  "company": "Name of the hiring company",
  "title": "Job title/role being advertised",
  "location": "Work location including remote/hybrid status",
  "type": "Employment type (Full-time, Contract, etc)",
  "salary": "Compensation details if provided",
  "description": "Main job description text including requirements and team info",
  "apply_url": "Direct application/job posting URL",
  "company_url": "Company website or careers page"
}
```

## Clean the result 

```json
{
"title": "Ask HN: Who is hiring? (February 2025)",
"createdAt": "2025-02-03T16:00:43Z",
"updatedAt": "2025-02-17T08:35:44Z",
"storyId": "42919502"
},
{
"title": "Ask HN: Who is hiring? (January 2025)",
"createdAt": "2025-01-02T16:00:09Z",
"updatedAt": "2025-02-13T00:03:24Z",
"storyId": "42575537"
},
```

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
airtable Token  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Search for Who is hiring posts" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get latest post" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split out children (jobs)" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Trun into structured data" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Limit for testing (optional)" node (n8n-nodes-base.limit) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Write results to airtable" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HI API: Get the individual job post" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HN API: Get Main Post" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Get relevant data".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Extract text".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
